title: An original resolution authorizing expenditures by the Committee on Foreign Relations.
plain_summary: This bill gives the Senate Committee on Foreign Relations permission to spend money over about two years, from March 2025 to February 2027. The committee gets the most money in 2026, which is over ten million dollars. They can use this money to pay workers, hire experts to help them, train their staff, and buy things they need for their offices. All of this money comes from the Senate's budget.
last_action_text: Referred to the Committee on Rules and Administration. (text: CR S1314)

sections.title: Section 1 General Authority
sections.text: The Committee on Foreign Relations is allowed to do its job according to Senate rules from March first two thousand twenty five through February twenty eighth two thousand twenty seven. The committee can spend money from the Senate's emergency fund, hire staff, and borrow workers from other government departments or agencies if those departments agree and the Committee on Rules and Administration says it is okay. The committee can also hold hearings, write reports about those hearings, and investigate issues as allowed by Senate rules.

sections.title: Section 2 Expenses
sections.text: From March first two thousand twenty five through September thirtieth two thousand twenty five, the committee can spend up to six million sixty eight thousand two hundred eighty nine dollars. Of this amount, no more than two hundred fifty thousand dollars can be used to hire outside experts or consulting companies. No more than thirty thousand dollars can be used to train the committee's professional staff. From October first two thousand twenty five through September thirtieth two thousand twenty six, the committee can spend up to ten million four hundred two thousand seven hundred eighty one dollars. The same limits apply for hiring consultants and training staff. From October first two thousand twenty six through February twenty eighth two thousand twenty seven, the committee can spend up to four million three hundred thirty four thousand four hundred ninety two dollars. The same limits for consultants and training apply to this period as well.

sections.title: Section 3 Expenses and Agency Contributions
sections.text: The committee's expenses will be paid from the Senate's emergency fund when the committee chairman approves the payment requests. However, the committee does not need special approval forms for certain regular payments. These include paying yearly salaries to employees, phone and internet services from the Senate's security office, office supplies from the Senate supply keeper, postage costs, copying machine charges, recording and photography services, and mailing costs handled by the Senate security office. The Senate can also pay money from its investigation budget to cover employee benefits for committee workers during all three time periods mentioned in this resolution.
